es.text: Con las manos en la masa
es.literal: with the hands in the dough
es.example: Lo agarraron con las manos en la masa.

it.literal: with the hands in the sack
it.example: L'hanno colto con le mani nel sacco.

bridge.note: Both freeze the thief mid-reach, but into different containers: Spanish catches hands in the dough (the baker's kitchen), Italian in the sack (the granary). English opts for the colour of blood. Three cultures, one incriminating posture.

interference.es_to_it: 'Con le mani nella pasta' points Italians at dinner, not crime — the fixed form is nel sacco. Note the verb: cogliere/colto.
interference.it_to_es: 'Con las manos en el saco' is not the Spanish idiom; the dough (la masa) does the incriminating.

review.notes: Discovered via FluentU list (Joe, 2026-07-16); all content authored fresh, ES pairing by Claude — verify.
